summary: Gruber announces the release of SmartyPants 1.5, noting minor improvements and teasing integration with an unannounced new plug-in coming soon. He playfully hints that readers should check the release notes if they want to be early adopters. An update follows announcing version 1.5.1, which fixes a bug related to the not-yet-announced plug-in causing a blank menu item in Movable Type. The post is notable primarily as a teaser for what turned out to be Markdown, as indicated by the 'Next' link.
key_points: SmartyPants 1.5 released with minor improvements and integration hooks for an upcoming plug-in
The post serves as a deliberate teaser for Markdown, Gruber's most influential creation
A quick 1.5.1 bugfix followed to address a blank menu item caused by the unreleased plug-in's absence
Gruber uses playful secrecy to build anticipation for the announcement
money_quotes: Just a couple of minor improvements — plus one more thing.
Check the release notes if you can't wait for the next announcement, or if you want to be able to tell your grandchildren you were in on it from before it was actually announced.
key_insight: This brief teaser post is historically significant as the immediate precursor to the announcement of Markdown, with Gruber borrowing Steve Jobs's 'one more thing' cadence to build anticipation for what would become one of the most widely adopted text formatting tools on the web.
